contract.title: US grants license for new nuclear reactor in 2026?
contract.resolution_criteria:
This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC)
issues a new combined license (COL) for the construction and operation of a nuclear
power plant by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. A combined license must be clearly
identified as such and documented in official NRC releases. Only initial issuances
count; amendments, renewals, or partial approvals do not qualify.

Note: In March 2026, TerraPower's Natrium sodium-cooled advanced reactor received
its first commercial construction permit — the first non-light-water reactor permit
in years — but a separate combined operating license (COL) application remains
pending. This market resolves on the full COL issuance, not the construction permit.

conditions.label: NRC issues initial COL by deadline
conditions.summary: By Dec 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, the U.S. NRC issues an initial combined license (COL) to construct and operate a nuclear power plant, in an official NRC release. No amendments/renewals/partials.

contract.narrative: Since March 2026, multistate AGs led by California AG Bonta oppose federal efforts to exempt new/experimental reactors from environmental review, creating legal and political headwinds that could delay NRC combined-license approvals this year. Other listed items (Ontario securities rule and CA oil-pipeline case) are unrelated and don’t affect odds. NRC licensing timelines and any court outcomes on the exemptions remain pending.

timeline.title: Attorney General Bonta Seeks to Halt Trump Administration’s Illegal Greenlight of Oil Transportation in California Pipelines
timeline.one_line_why: California AG's action targets oil pipeline approvals, unrelated to NRC combined-license process, so it doesn't affect COL issuance odds.

timeline.title: Attorney General Bonta Seeks to Halt Trump Administration’s Illegal Greenlight of Oil Transportation - May 1, 2026
timeline.regulator: California Department of Justice, Office of the Attorney General
timeline.one_line_why: California AG action on oil transport is unrelated to NRC combined-license issuance and does not affect COL odds.

timeline.title: Notice of General Order – Ontario Instrument 33-510 Exemptions from Requirements to Submit Certain Personal Information under OSC Rule 33-506 (Commodity Futures Act) Registration Information
timeline.one_line_why: Ontario Securities Commission exemptions are unrelated to U.S. NRC combined licenses and have no bearing on COL issuance probability.

timeline.title: Attorney General Bonta Opposes Trump Administration’s Attempt to Exempt New and Experimental Nuclear Reactors from Environmental Review
timeline.one_line_why: Multi-state AG opposition to federal exemption of environmental review raises legal and political hurdles, lowering odds and likely delaying an NRC COL.

timeline.title: Attorney General Bonta Opposes Trump Administration’s Attempt to Exempt Nuclear Reactors - March 5, 2026
timeline.one_line_why: California AG's opposition to federal exemptions could block expedited reviews and make an NRC COL by end-2026 less likely.

primary_drivers: Repeated legal filings by California AG Bonta
Overall decline in mentions this week
heat_panel.explainer: Dormant heat reflects a handful of repeated CA AG Bonta legal actions plus an Ontario exemptions notice, with overall mentions dropping this week.
